title: A small test site for a much more flexible CMS
intro: This page exists to exercise singleton JSON content, nested collections, markdown fields, and a reusable gallery block all in one place.
body:
## Why this fixture exists

Tentman just got a serious content-system refactor, so this site is here to give us something a little more realistic than placeholder data.

- The about page is a JSON singleton.
- The blog is a directory-backed collection.
- The gallery below uses a reusable block definition.

## What to try

Edit the copy, swap images, add a highlight card, then make sure those changes show up here without any extra glue code.

highlights.label: Content modes
highlights.detail: Single-file JSON for pages and directory-backed entries for posts.

highlights.label: Reusable blocks
highlights.detail: The same image gallery block can be dropped into any page config.

highlights.label: Docs coverage
highlights.detail: The plans folder and in-app docs page describe the schema this fixture is using.

gallery.alt: Pinned planning notes and sketches on a studio wall
gallery.caption: Planning docs and prototypes from the recent refactor work.

gallery.alt: A bright desk with a keyboard, coffee, and design comps
gallery.caption: A calm editing surface makes it easier to test real authoring flows.

gallery.alt: A workshop board with cards arranged in lanes
gallery.caption: This reusable gallery is a good stress test for nested structured values.
